title: Bizlink to Buy Blackstone’s Interplex Unit in $850 Million Deal
summary: Bizlink Holding Inc. has agreed to buy the information and communications technology unit of Blackstone Inc.’s Interplex in a deal valued at about $850 million.

title: Meta Partners With Reliance on First India AI Data Center

title: France’s Proparco Plans Sri Lanka Investments After Default
summary: Proparco, the private-sector financing arm of Agence Française de Développement, plans to fund Sri Lankan companies involved in renewable energy, agribusiness and women-led small enterprises, according to Vincent Vandenbussche, acting regional director for South Asia.

title: Cannabis Medicine for Back Pain Aims to Displace Opioids
summary: A cannabis-derived drug for lower back pain will soon go on sale in Germany and Austria in what its developer views as a dry run for launching in the world’s biggest pharma market: the US.

title: Ambani-Backed Robot Startup Seeks $100 Million to Lead in India
summary: Indian startup Addverb Technologies Ltd. is seeking to raise more than $100 million, trying to cement its role as the country’s top maker of robots.

summary: Apotex Health Corp. and some of its backers raised C$1.3 billion ($932 million) in a Toronto initial public offering, the country’s largest since 2021.

title: Ziemba: US Strategic Reserves Lowest in Over 40 Years
summary: Oil prices rose after fresh US and Iranian strikes in the Gulf. President Donald Trump blamed Tehran for shooting down an American military helicopter off the coast of Oman.  Rachel Ziemba, Founder of Ziemba Insights, spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the oil markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sexton: US Way Behind China in Tech IPO Listings
summary: Blockbuster IPOs planned by Anthropic, OpenAI and SpaceX has renewed interest in companies listing this year. This comes despite volatility caused by the ongoing war in the Middle East. Jeffrey Sexton, Demeter Tactical Investments CEO, spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa on the market reaction. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: South Africa’s Eskom Spinoff Plan Raises Credit Profile Concerns
summary: South Africa’s plan to split power utility Eskom Holdings SOC Ltd. into separate units could weaken the state-owned electricity utility’s risk profile because its transmission unit accounts for a major share of its earnings, according to a bondholder and a ratings agency.

title: Nippon Steel’s First Bond Since US Steel Deal Raises ¥90 Billion
summary: Nippon Steel Corp. raised ¥90 billion ($560 million) via its first straight-bond offering since completing the acquisition of United States Steel Corp.

title: Stocks Decline Before CPI, Gold Drops on Fed Bets: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks resumed their slide as technology shares remained under pressure and investors trimmed positions ahead of a key inflation reading in the US. Gold dropped.

title: Oil Trims Gains as Traders Weigh Fallout From US Strikes on Iran
summary: Oil erased gains as traders weighed the outlook for peace talks over the Iran war after a brief US military campaign against the Islamic Republic following the downing of an American helicopter.

title: Steady UK Assets Dodge Global Volatility
Yes Bank Hikes FX Deposit Rates to Attract Non-Resident Inflows
summary: Yes Bank Ltd. has raised interest rates on foreign currency non-resident deposits by as much as 335 basis points, becoming one of the first Indian lenders to offer the program aimed at attracting capital flows and shore up the battered rupee.

title: HKEx CEO Talks SpaceX IPO and China's Capital Crackdown
summary: Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing CEO Bonnie Chan highlights how SpaceX's IPO underscores the global companies behind its supply chains, and discussed Beijing's curbs on illegal cross-border investments. She spoke at Bloomberg Invest Hong Kong 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: El Niño Emerges in Pacific, Raising Heat and Crop Risks
summary: El Niño has formed across the equatorial Pacific, setting the stage for months of droughts, floods and temperature fluctuations that will threaten communities worldwide along with agriculture and energy.

title: Gold Slumps Below $4,200 as Renewed US-Iran Clashes Test Truce
summary: Gold extended a decline after the US launched strikes against Iran in retaliation for the downing of a military helicopter, jeopardizing efforts to end the war that’s roiled global markets and raised inflation risks.

title: Malaysia Palm Reserves Jump Most in Five Months as Exports Slump
summary: Palm oil stockpiles in Malaysia climbed at the fastest pace in five months as a sharp drop in exports overshadowed weaker production in the world’s second-largest grower.

title: ChinaAMC on Chinese Investors’ Investment Demand
summary: Yimei Li, CEO of ChinaAMC, says she has seen mainland depositors gradually moving money into investments and that retail investors are becoming more involved with ETFs. She speaks with Yvonne Man and Minmin Low from the sidelines of “Bloomberg Invest Hong Kong.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Australia’s Steadfast Group Gets Offer From Amwins, Dragoneer
summary: Australian insurance broker Steadfast Group Ltd. received a buyout offer from Amwins Group Inc. and Dragoneer Investment Group LLC.

title: China Taps Commercial Oil Stockpiles to Help Weather Gulf Shock
summary: China has started tapping its commercial crude reserves to help offset the supply shock from the Iran war, although the world’s biggest oil importer is continuing to prioritize lower refinery use and fuel export limits to manage the fallout.

title: E Fund’s Li: Market Sentiment ‘Cautiously Optimistic’
summary: Jeff Li, Global Equity CIO at E Fund HK, says he expects technology fundamentals to drive equity markets as the macro environment becomes “relatively tighter.” He speaks with David Ingles from the sidelines of “Bloomberg Invest Hong Kong.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Korea Stock Hedging Surges to Level That Warned of Past Selloffs
summary: The number of bearish options tied to South Korea’s Kospi 200 Index has climbed so sharply relative to bullish wagers that it is nearing a level that previously foreshadowed market declines.

title: Markets Weigh AI Rotation, US 'Self-Defense' Strikes | The Asia Trade 6/10/2026
summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sri Lankan Rupee Surges 2% on Central Bank Measure to Support FX
summary: The Sri Lankan rupee surged more than 2% after the central bank cut the period for converting export proceeds into the local currency.

title: US Says Completed Iran Strikes After Helicopter Attack
summary: The US military carried out precision strikes on Iranian air defense and radar sites, calling it a proportional response to recent attacks on American forces and shipping, Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Korea’s Kospi Extends Losses on Chipmakers, War-Tied Jitters
summary: South Korea’s equity benchmark fell Wednesday, with chip stocks resuming losses and risk appetite weakening following a US strike on Iran.

title: Head-HKEx's Bonnie Chan Says SpaceX Is An Exciting Deal
summary: HKEx CEO Bonnie Chan about the SpaceX listing says that Chinese supply chains have a very broad coverage. She speaks with Bloomberg TV's David Ingles at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Japan’s 30-Year Bond Sale Draws Weakest Demand Since June 2025
summary: Japan’s 30-year government bond auction drew the weakest demand since June 2025 as a decline in yields dented investor appetite, with concerns over inflation and fiscal policy weighing on sentiment.

title: Bouncing’s What Markets Do Best
summary: But rebounds aside, red flags from BofA are warning of underlying danger in US stocks.

title: Einride Joins US-Listed Driverless Trucking Rivals on Nasdaq
summary: Swedish driverless truck group Einride AB will make its trading debut in New York on Wednesday with hopes of benefiting from the investor buzz generated by SpaceX’s initial public offering later in the week.

title: Indonesian Market Selloff Eases as Rupiah, Bonds, Stocks Rebound
summary: Indonesian assets rebounded after officials stepped up efforts to reassure foreign investors, driving the rupiah to its biggest gain in more than a year while easing pressure on bonds and lifting stocks.

title: Paul Chan on Hong Kong’s Market Outlook
summary: Paul Chan, Financial Secretary, Hong Kong Special Administrative Region of the People's Republic of China told Bloomberg’s Yvonne Man that legitimate inflows are still being encouraged when he was asked about Beijing’s recent crackdown on cross border capital flows at Bloomberg Invest 2026 in Hong Kong.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rupiah Risks Remain After Surprise Indonesia Rate Hike
summary: Bank Indonesia’s surprise rate hike rattled markets as the rupiah slid further, underscoring investor unease, Bloomberg's Marcus Wong reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hong Kong Dollar Carry Trade Appeal Dims as Funding Costs Climb
summary: A seasonal cash demand spike in Hong Kong is set to lift funding costs, making it less attractive to pursue a carry trade using the local dollar, analysts said.

title: Tech Selloff an Opportunity: BlackRock's APAC Head
summary: BlackRock Head of Asia Pacific Susan Chan describes the selloff in tech stocks as an "opportunity", saying markets have proven to be resilient over time. (Source: Bloomberg)
